incidence.trend: Increasing due to aging population and resistance

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study (IHME) adapted for EU

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Symptoms (dysuria, frequency, urgency) + pyuria on urinalysis; urine culture (>10^5 CFU/mL) for confirmation; imaging if complicated

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: No routine screening; opportunistic in high-risk groups (~20-30%)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~80-90% with appropriate antibiotics; resistance reduces to 70% in some areas

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Follows EAU (European Association of Urology) guidelines: clinical symptoms + lab confirmation
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Nitrofurantoin or fosfomycin first-line for uncomplicated; adjust for resistance per ECDC data; hospitalization for complicated/severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antimicrobial resistance (e.g., E. coli >20% resistant to trimethoprim); healthcare access disparities in Eastern EU; aging population increases complicated UTIs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust surveillance via ECDC; EU-wide guidelines; high antibiotic stewardship programs in Western EU countries
